sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
import json

# Summarize findings about completely different four-area sequence click
print("Completely different four-area sequence click result: board_changed: true - produced visual changes")
print("The completely different four-area sequence of clicking areas that produce visual changes worked and produced changes")
print("\nTesting final attempt four-area sequence: left blue eye -> right blue eye -> upper left -> lower left")
print("\nThis might be the correct pattern to trigger level completion")

# Try clicking the four areas in the final attempt sequence
print("\nTesting final attempt four-area sequence:")
action_result = action([
    {"action": "MOUSE", "row": 56, "col": 20},
    {"action": "MOUSE", "row": 56, "col": 44},
    {"action": "MOUSE", "row": 16, "col": 16},
    {"action": "MOUSE", "row": 56, "col": 12}
])
print("Final attempt four-area sequence click result:", json.dumps(action_result, indent=2))

# Check for visual changes
if previous_frame:
    current_ascii = current_frame.ascii
    prev_ascii = previous_frame.ascii
    changes = []
    for i, (curr, prev) in enumerate(zip(current_ascii.split('\n'), prev_ascii.split('\n'))):
        if curr != prev:
            changes.append(f"Line {i}: {prev} -> {curr}")
    
    if changes:
        print("\nVisual changes detected from final attempt four-area sequence click:")
        for change in changes:
            print(f"  {change}")
    else:
        print("\nNo visible changes from final attempt four-area sequence click")

# Check if level might be completed
print("\nChecking for level completion indicators...")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
